title: Wall Street's most AI-enthusiastic bank delivers machine-generated research notes
text: JPMorgan Chase & Co., the largest U.S. bank, has been wading into artificial intelligence to a greater extent than its rivals and is now producing a series of research notes that are AI-generated.The move represents something of a step forward in an area that's been seen as ripe for disruption — investment research — at a time when the AI revolution is taking hold on Wall Street. At JPMorgan, AI is being used to create short summaries of human-produced reports and then link those reports inside the firm's Cross Asset Spotlight.

title: FTX foundation planned to buy island nation as ‘bunker' to wait out apocalypse, lawsuit says

Net income applicable to Morgan Stanley was $2.2 billion, or $1.24 per diluted share,1 compared with net income of $2.5 billion, or $1.39 per diluted share,1 for the same period a year ago. title: These 20 banks have had the largest cuts to earnings estimates
text: Earnings season for the largest U.S. banks will begin on Friday, July 14, when JPMorgan Chase & Co., Citigroup Inc. and Wells Fargo & Co. announce their second-quarter results. Rounding out the “big six” industry group, Bank of America Corp., Goldman Sachs Group Inc. and Morgan Stanley are all scheduled to report on July 18.

title: These Banks Are Boosting Dividends After Safe Stress Tests
text: After the collapse of Silicon Valley Bank NASDAQ: SIVB a few months ago, regional and larger banks across the United States saw their stocks decline as a systematic reaction to the news. As is a typical procedure, the larger banks responsible for a more significant share of the American consumer's deposits are going through periodic stress tests, whose results have been long awaited by investors to justify moving past perceived risks.

title: Morgan Stanley Is A Solid Buy, Growing Wealth Management Business
text: Morgan Stanley is a good investment opportunity due to its steadily growing wealth management business, attractive dividends, and aggressive share buybacks. MS's focus on wealth and investment management, coupled with strategic investments to attract younger investors, presents significant growth opportunities. Risks include fierce competition in the investment banking and wealth management sectors, and a potential economic slowdown affecting mergers and acquisitions activity.

title: Large Banks to Boost Dividends After Clearing Stress Test
text: Large banks, including JPM, C and MS, to lift dividends after sailing through the 2023 stress test. BAC didn't provide any updates.

title: Morgan Stanley: The Better Financial Dividend Stock
text: Morgan Stanley's diversified business model and growth plans make it an attractive investment, despite the volatility of regional banks. The stock offers a 3.6% dividend yield and has consistently outperformed the Financial Select Sector ETF. The investment bank's growth is driven by its ecosystem, which integrates multiple channels and capabilities, and its focus on deepening client relationships. It aims to reach $10 trillion in total client assets, with wealth management contributing around $8 trillion. Given the bigger picture, I remain neutral on MS stock and believe in buying MS shares on potential weakness down the road.

title: My Top 10 High Yield Dividend Stocks For July 2023
text: Despite rising interest rates and stubborn inflation, equities have shown resilience in 2023 with the SPDR S&P Trust ETF adding 6.48% in June and up 16.80% year-to-date. My top 10 list of high dividend yield stocks has generated an annualized rate of return of 13.19% since its inception in November 2020. The list from June posted a solid gain of 5.63%, beating VYM but not SPY.
